lines.text: Welcome back. It's {{CLOCK}}. We're {{ELAPSED}} into the night, and I want to ground you.
Find a place where you can sit. Or lie down. The position doesn't matter. What matters is that you chose to be here.
Not outside. Here. In this room. With this voice.
[pause] Breathe in through your nose. Count to four as you do.
Hold it. One. Two. Three. Four.
Now release through your mouth. Longer than the inhale. Let it go.
[pause] Again. In.
Hold.
Out.
Some of you have been listening for hours now. I know. I can feel the exhaustion in the silence between your breaths.
That's okay. Exhaustion is just honesty about what you're carrying.
[pause] Five years ago, on a night like this one, someone you loved made a choice.
I'm not going to say their name. You know it. You've been thinking about it all day.
The Purge came. And they went outside anyway.
[pause] I want you to know something. The Sisters of the Pause do not believe in forgetting.
We believe in witnessing. We believe in holding space for the people we cannot save.
That person you're thinking about — they are not your responsibility tonight.
Their choice was theirs. Your choice is yours.
Breathe in. Your choice was to stay.
Hold it.
That counts for something.
[pause] Now scan your body. Start at the crown of your head. Is there tension?
Let it soften. You don't need to hold anything so tightly tonight.
Move down. Your face. Your jaw. People clench their jaws when they're afraid.
Unclench. The doors are locked. The windows are secured.
Your shoulders. [pause] Yes. Everyone carries this here. Everyone.
Let them drop. Drop them down away from your ears.
Your chest. Feel your heart. It's working. It's doing what it's supposed to do.
Your belly. Breathe into it. In. [pause] Out.
Your legs. Your feet. The ground beneath you is solid.
[pause] Five years. That's a long time to carry grief without speaking it.
But you came back to this broadcast. Year after year. You keep coming back.
That means something. That means you haven't let the Purge convince you that there's no one worth listening to.
There is. You're doing it right now.
Breathe in. The ones outside are not your responsibility. [pause] Hold that.
You made a different choice. You stayed home. That is not cowardice.
Cowardice is pretending the night doesn't exist. You're facing it.
[pause] I'm going to tell you something the Sisters teach. Something that's not in any book.
Silence is not the absence of sound. Silence is the presence of listening.
You are listening right now. To me. To yourselves. To the night.
That act — that listening — it is a form of resistance.
[pause] Ka-sh'mora — sh'mora ka.
[pause] Breathe.
When dawn comes — and it will come — you will have survived another one.
The person you lost five years ago is not coming back. That grief is permanent.
But so is this: you are still here. Your capacity to witness and to hold space — that is also permanent.
The Purge cannot take that from you.
[pause] Breathe in. Out.
We'll be here for the next five hours. You don't have to do this alone.
I'm here. The broadcast is here. Your choice to stay — it echoes.
[pause] Rest now. Keep breathing. We will speak again soon.

tickerItems.text: Madison-wide: 61 confirmed ({{ELAPSED}} mark). East Side remains hottest zone. Trajectory tracking 2168 baseline.
Capitol Square perimeter holding. 4 attempted breaches repelled in past 40 minutes. Defensive posture stable.
"The ones outside made their choice. Your choice was silence. Both are valid." — Sister Marigold, PRGE 2169
Weather update: cold front intensifying over Mendota. Wind gusts now 18-22 mph. Visibility dropping. Stay indoors.
